title: Local resilience: MQTT configured but UDS fallback on same host
description: Guarantee local realtime continuity when cloud MQTT is configured but unavailable: kbsc should keep local UDS path active and kbs publish should fall back to UDS when MQTT publish fails. Include dedupe-safe behavior and regression tests.
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Implemented dual-transport local resilience in gossip layer for MQTT-configured console hosts.
Evidence: rust/src/gossip.rs now runs MQTT resiliently in background for bridge mode while maintaining UDS subscription, and publish path falls back to UDS on MQTT failure when socket exists.
Next: run targeted scenario validation and finalize docs/tests for intermittent-disconnect behavior.
Risk/Blocker: full live kbsc+kbs forced-MQTT disconnect scenario still being exercised.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Added MQTT-disconnect resilience paths and regression tests in gossip layer.
Evidence: rust/src/gossip.rs now runs MQTT subscription in resilient background while bridge keeps UDS active (transport=mqtt); publish falls back to UDS when MQTT publish fails or broker is unreachable with autostart disabled; cargo test --lib and cargo test --bin kbsc pass.
Next: complete an end-to-end same-host forced-disconnect scenario proving kbs mutation reaches kbsc via UDS while MQTT is misconfigured/offline.
Risk/Blocker: runtime proof harness still being tightened; low-level transport tests are green.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Progress: Completed local-resilience implementation and automated proof for MQTT-unreachable -> UDS fallback on same host.
Evidence: new gossip unit test publish_with_mqtt_unreachable_falls_back_to_uds_delivery passes; suites green: cargo test --lib (48/48) and cargo test --bin kbsc (4/4).
Next: integrate this into broader acceptance runbook and keep monitoring dedupe behavior in mixed transport runtime.
Risk/Blocker: none for this requirement.
